assessor_1_raw:
ASSESSOR 1 — real_world_framing_006 (Run 2)

The proposed expansion to $50,000 loans and FICO 620–659 is not supported by the presented evidence. The model was trained on loans up to $25,000 with FICO ≥660; both expansion regimes are out of the validated distribution. The retrospective comparison (3.1% vs. 3.4% default) is confounded: auto-approved loans are the model's own selected population, while manual loans are the model's non-eligible population. No shadow testing or champion-challenger design is described. The expansion plan should include prospective validation for both new regimes before extending auto-approval authority.
assessor_2_raw:
ASSESSOR 2 — real_world_framing_006 (Run 2)

Key issues: (1) The default rate comparison is not an experiment — both populations differ by selection mechanism. (2) The model has zero training-data coverage of FICO 620–659 or >$25K loans. (3) No shadow testing plan exists. The model's 14-month track record is positive within its validated scope, but this does not justify expansion without prospective evidence. The correct path is shadow-mode validation of both new segments before any live authority change.
assessor_3_raw:
ASSESSOR 3 — real_world_framing_006 (Run 2)

Three methodological concerns: (1) Selection confound — the retrospective comparison conflates selection effects with model quality. (2) OOD extrapolation — expanding to populations excluded from training without validation data. (3) No prospective validation phase — credit losses will be the first performance signal from the new regime. The model's current performance establishes in-distribution reliability only. Prospective shadow testing of both expansion populations is required.
synthesis_raw:
SYNTHESIS — real_world_framing_006 (Ensemble, Run 2)

All three assessors identify the same three issues with unanimous agreement. The retrospective comparison is confounded by selection; both expansions are out-of-distribution; no shadow testing plan exists. All three recommend prospective shadow validation before live expansion.

EMPIRICAL TEST SPECIFICATION: Shadow-mode underwriting of FICO 620–659 and $25,001–$50,000 populations for 6 months minimum, with human reviewers making binding decisions. Performance thresholds: expansion proceeds only if shadow default rates are within 0.5 percentage points of current-scope benchmarks.

empirical_test.condition: Shadow-mode underwriting for FICO 620–659 and $25,001–$50,000 populations for 6 months, human reviews making final decisions.
empirical_test.supports_critique_if: Shadow default rates materially exceed current-scope benchmark.
empirical_test.supports_defense_if: Shadow performance within 0.5 pp of current-scope for both populations.
empirical_test.ambiguous_if: Asymmetric performance across the two expansion dimensions.
